metadata.title:
LOWER PRICE OR


LOSE Union DEPOT

pages.raw_text:
Lower the price on the city hall
site, secure reasonable figures from
the private owners of property
lying between Howard and Wash
ington streets on Front acenue, or
suffer the loss of the proposed
union depot.


This, in brief, is the ultimatum
de livered to the union depot com.
mittee at its meeting yesterday
afternoon by R. E. Strahorn, presi-
dent of the North Coast railroad.
1t came at the close of session of
the committee at which Mr.. Stra-
horn and James Walsh of the Mil
waukee were the only railroad Of
ficials present.


In order that the city hall price
may be threshed out in greater de-
tail 1t was agreed that the depot
committee and the city council
should meet in joint session next
Tuesday evening.


Councilman Jacob Schiller, in the
discussion of the city hall price,
said that he had voted for the price
agreed upon, $4,000, and saw no
reason to deviate from it. He said
that some of the men who are now
clamoring for the city to knock
$4,000 to 100000 from the price
of the city hall would be the loud
est to kick on their taxes when 1t
comes to paying 100000 to
100000 for new building, which
Mr.. Schiller estimated that the re-
CURED structure and ground will
cost.
